task_completion.description: The maintenance request spreadsheet is opened in LibreOffice Calc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc is open, displaying columns such as Request Date, Unit #, Issue Description, Status, Assigned To, Cost, and Days Open.

task_completion.description: The 'Days Open' column is populated with calculated values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Days Open' column (typically column G) contains numerical values representing the number of days elapsed, rather than being empty.

task_completion.description: Conditional formatting is applied to highlight overdue items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the 'Days Open' column (and potentially the 'Status' column) have distinct background or text colors applied based on their values (e.g., larger numbers in 'Days Open' are highlighted in red).

task_completion.description: A summary statistics section is created for management reporting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new section or table is visible (often below the main data or to the side) containing summary labels (e.g., 'Total Requests', 'Open', 'Overdue') and aggregated numerical values.

task_completion.description: The updated workbook is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Calc window title bar displays 'maintenance_tracker.xlsx', indicating the file has been saved with the correct name.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc graphical interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through LibreOffice Calc menus, dialogs (like Conditional Formatting), and typing in cells.

integrity.description: The original maintenance request data is preserved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original ~18 rows of data (dates, unit numbers, descriptions) are still present and have not been deleted or replaced by dummy text.

integrity.description: The agent used actual formulas rather than hardcoding values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar shows actual Excel/Calc formulas (e.g., =TODAY(), =COUNTIF(), =SUM()) when summary cells or 'Days Open' cells are selected.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying a spreadsheet with ~18 rows of maintenance requests. Key requirements to verify: 1) 'Days Open' column must be populated with time-based calculations (typically using TODAY()). 2) Conditional formatting must be applied to highlight overdue items in the 'Days Open' column (the verifier also checks for Status column formatting). 3) A summary statistics section must be present, using functions like COUNT, SUM, COUNTIF, or COUNTIFS to aggregate data. 4) The original data must be preserved (at least 15 rows of data). 5) The file must be saved as 'maintenance_tracker.xlsx'.
